name: Finlight Financial News API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and terminology for the Finlight financial news and sentiment analysis API

terms.definition: A financial news article ingested from a publisher's website, enriched with AI-driven sentiment analysis, category classification, and company entity extraction.
A publicly listed company identified and extracted from article content, associated with financial identifiers such as ticker symbol, ISIN, and exchange listing.
A specific exchange listing for a company, identified by ticker symbol, exchange code (MIC), and country.
A financial news publisher identified by domain name, with metadata indicating whether full content is available and whether it is included in the default source set.
An AI-generated classification of whether article content is positive, negative, or neutral with respect to financial markets or the mentioned companies.
A numeric score between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating the model's certainty in its sentiment classification or entity extraction result.
An abbreviation used to uniquely identify a publicly traded company on a stock exchange (e.g., AAPL for Apple Inc., NVDA for NVIDIA Corporation).
International Securities Identification Number - a 12-character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a financial instrument globally (e.g., US0378331005).
Open Financial Instrument Global Identifier - a globally unique identifier for financial instruments maintained by Bloomberg.
A news source included in the standard source set returned when no explicit sources filter is specified in an API request.
An advanced query string supporting boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT), field filters (ticker:AAPL, source:reuters.com), and phrase matching ("exact phrase").
A real-time streaming protocol connection to Finlight's live article feed, enabling sub-second latency delivery of new financial news.
An HTTP callback delivery mechanism where Finlight POSTs article data to a user-configured endpoint when new articles are published, secured with HMAC-SHA256 signatures.
The process of identifying and tagging companies mentioned in an article, associating them with structured data including ticker, ISIN, exchange, industry, and sector.
A content classification label assigned to an article indicating its primary topic area within financial news.
A non-default news source that can be included in results by explicitly specifying it in the optInSources parameter.
A security threat where a captured webhook request is maliciously re-sent; mitigated by Finlight's X-Webhook-Timestamp header with a 5-minute tolerance window.
Hash-based Message Authentication Code using SHA-256 hashing, used to sign webhook payloads so recipients can verify authenticity.
Model Context Protocol - Finlight provides an MCP server for integrating financial news data directly into AI assistant workflows.
